definition: Global pension assets ~$55-60T with ~60% in DB plans requiring duration matching create a structural bid for long-duration assets. This demand is mandate-driven (ALM), not behavioral, and reverses only with regulatory change. The pension bid now collides with rising fiscal supply from aging-related spending — forces that reinforced each other from 2000-2020 have diverged.
evidence: Global pension assets ~$55-60T, ~60% in DB plans requiring duration matching
UK DB pensions ~GBP 1.5T in gilts/LDI; US DB pensions ~$5T in fixed income
UK LDI crisis (2022) demonstrated structural bid is leveraged and fragile
Pension demand and fiscal supply reinforced each other 2000-2020, now diverge
Mandate-driven (ALM), not behavioral — reverses only with regulatory change
open_questions: Which force dominates at different points on the yield curve?
Is the 10Y-30Y segment the fulcrum for marginal shifts?
How does the DB-to-DC transition alter the structural demand profile over time?
